instruction: Use Mode 1 to create a new plan: given a PRD or requirements document, produce a complete, navigable implementation plan written to `.context/plans/plan-<slug>/`
original_snippets: Mode 1 — Create new plan: Given a PRD or requirements document, produce a complete, navigable implementation plan written to `.context/plans/`
relevant_when: User asks to create an implementation plan, break down a PRD, or decompose requirements into phases/tasks
why_given: Core mode 1 behaviour — agent must produce the correct directory structure

instruction: Use Mode 2 to restructure existing plans: given a monolithic document or flat set of phase files, produce a hierarchical directory structure
original_snippets: Mode 2 — Restructure Existing Plans: Use this mode when the source is a monolithic document or flat set of phase files that needs to be reorganised into a navigable hierarchy
relevant_when: User asks to split, reorganise, or refactor an existing planning document
why_given: Core mode 2 behaviour — agent must recognise restructuring trigger and apply correct workflow

instruction: Root `README.md` is a navigation index only; do not embed implementation detail in it
original_snippets: Do not embed implementation detail in `plan.md` — keep it as a navigation index only. Detail belongs in phase and task files
relevant_when: Always in Mode 1 when writing the root README
why_given: Prevents agents from bloating the root index with task-level content

instruction: Each phase must have a clear goal statement, a gate (pass/fail acceptance criteria verifiable by running commands), and explicit dependencies
original_snippets: Each phase must have: A clear goal statement; A gate (pass/fail acceptance criteria that can be verified by running commands); Explicit dependencies on prior phases or existing artefacts
why_given: Ensures phases are actionable and testable, not vague milestones

instruction: Each task must be completable in isolation, verifiable with a concrete shell command or observable output, and scoped to a single file or tightly coupled set of files
original_snippets: Each task must be: Completable in isolation (no hidden cross-task dependencies unless declared); Verifiable with a concrete shell command or observable output; Scoped to a single file or a tightly coupled set of files
relevant_when: Mode 1: when decomposing phases into tasks
why_given: Ensures tasks can be handed to an agent independently

instruction: Use identifier format `P{phase_number}T{task_number}` with zero-padded, 1-based numbering for consistent alphabetical sorting
original_snippets: Use the identifier format `P{phase_number}T{task_number}`, both zero-padded. Example: `P02T03` = phase 2, task 3. Use 1-based numbering (`01`, `02`, …) for consistent alphabetical sorting
why_given: Naming convention must be followed exactly for downstream tooling and sorting

instruction: Run the scaffold scripts to create the directory structure deterministically: `new-plan.sh`, `new-phase.sh`, `new-task.sh`; then populate generated stubs from templates
original_snippets: Use the scripts in `scripts/` to create the directory structure deterministically, then populate the generated README / task files using the templates in `references/templates/`
why_given: Scripts ensure consistent structure; agents must not hand-create directories without them

instruction: Run `validate-plan.sh <plan-slug>` after writing all files; fix any failure and re-run until exit 0 before reporting to the user
original_snippets: After writing all files, run the validation script to verify every file conforms to its JSON Schema before reporting to the user
If any file fails, fix it and re-run until all checks pass (exit 0) before proceeding
relevant_when: Mode 1 Step 5: after scaffolding is complete
why_given: Mandatory validation gate — agents must not skip this step

instruction: Every task file must include a verification section — a task is not complete without a provable, runnable check. Gates must never be vague (e.g. 'works correctly')
original_snippets: Every task file must include a verification section — a task is not complete without a provable, runnable check (exit 0 / non-zero, file exists, URL returns 200, etc.). Gates must never be vague like 'works correctly'
why_given: Vague gates are unverifiable; tasks need concrete pass/fail criteria

instruction: Ask the user before creating more than 8 phases
original_snippets: Ask the user before creating more than 8 phases — very large plans often benefit from being split into separate planning documents
relevant_when: Mode 1: when scope analysis suggests >8 phases
why_given: Prevents runaway plan creation without user confirmation

instruction: In Mode 2, never use numeric-only directory names or generic names; use descriptive kebab names that survive refactors
original_snippets: Never use numeric-only directory names (`step-1/`, `activity-2/`) and never use generic names (`step-1-stuff/`) — contributors cannot navigate without opening every file
why_given: Navigability requirement — directories must be self-descriptive

instruction: In Mode 2, apply flatten vs. subdivide heuristics: 3–7 items per group is the sweet spot; flatten groups with <3 children; subdivide groups with >10 children
original_snippets: Item has <3 children: Flatten
Item has >10 children: Subdivide
Rule of thumb: 3–7 items per group is the sweet spot
why_given: Structural heuristics that determine usable hierarchy depth

instruction: In Mode 2, create the new hierarchy first, validate with `validate-structure.sh`, then remove old flat files — never delete source before the new structure is confirmed valid
original_snippets: Create the new hierarchy first, validate, then remove the old flat files — never delete source before the new structure is confirmed valid

instruction: Slugs must be lowercase kebab-case summaries of the title
original_snippets: Slugs are lowercase kebab-case summaries of the title. Examples: Plan 'E-commerce Checkout Redesign' → `plan-ecommerce-checkout-redesign`
relevant_when: Mode 1: when naming all plan/phase/task files and directories
why_given: Consistent file naming required for script compatibility and sorting

instruction: Existing `.context/plans/` content is never deleted; new files are additive. If a plan already exists, append new phases rather than overwriting
original_snippets: Existing `.context/plans/` content is never deleted; new files are additive
If a plan already exists, append new phases rather than overwriting
relevant_when: Mode 1: when a plan directory already exists
why_given: Prevents accidental data loss on incremental plan additions

instruction: Report to user a structured summary listing all created files with phase task counts after writing all files
original_snippets: After writing all files, output a summary listing README.md, phases with task counts, and all task files
relevant_when: Mode 1: always as the final step
why_given: Completion reporting pattern — user needs to know what was created
